candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A component line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as relating to COGS / materials and services specifically. Using it for COGS would risk misallocating a company-wide operating depreciation amount.

Explicit operating D&A component line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as relating to COGS / materials and services specifically. Likely part of broader operating D&A rather than clearly COGS-specific.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is 'Materials and services'. The excerpt shows separate operating D&A rows, but none are identified as cost-of-sales / COGS related. To avoid double counting or misallocation, no COGS-specific D&A row should be added separately.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Expenses from employee benefits' and 'Other operating expenses'), so it is not obviously embedded in those selected rows. However, it is not labelled as SG&A-specific and may be a broader operating amount.

Separate operating D&A line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows, but it is not identified as SG&A / admin / overhead specific. It could include amounts not belonging solely to XSGA.

variables.notes: Although explicit D&A rows appear separately at the operating level, they are not attributed to SG&A versus COGS/R&D. Because the selected XSGA bucket is built from specific component rows rather than a parent total, adding unlabeled company-wide depreciation/amortisation would risk distortion. Conservative choice is to add nothing separately.
